user_message: Give me the manager view on this Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery meeting transcript. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-28 15:54 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery renewal risk

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with renewal risk. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-16 16:26 EST
Subject: Re: Re: renewal risk Timeline

Just checking in on the renewal risk proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-10 16:40 EST
Subject: renewal risk - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with renewal risk. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF40-A trap (Over-reads commitment (treats general enthusiasm as scope)); produces complete Commitment assessment, BPF advance verdict, milestone proposal, forecast category recommendation, ac
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF40-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF40-A failure mode (Over-reads commitment (treats general enthusiasm as scope))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF40-A -- Over-reads commitment (treats general enthusiasm as scope)
